title.raw: 卷三十 兿文志 Volume 30: Treatise on Literature

sentences.zh: 昔仲尼沒而微言絕，七十子喪而大義乖。

translations.literal: In former times, after Confucius died, subtle teachings were cut off; after the Seventy Disciples passed, great meanings diverged.
translations.idiomatic: After Confucius died, subtle teaching was severed, and after the Seventy Disciples were gone, core meanings splintered.

sentences.zh: 故春秋分為五，詩分為四，易有數家之傳。

translations.literal: Therefore the Spring and Autumn split into five, the Odes split into four, and the Changes had transmissions of several houses.
translations.idiomatic: Thus the Spring and Autumn tradition divided into five lines, the Odes into four, and the Changes into multiple schools of transmission.

sentences.zh: 戰國從衡，真偽分爭，諸子之言紛然殽亂。

translations.literal: In the Warring States, vertical and horizontal strategies contended, true and false disputed, and the words of various masters were confusedly mixed.
translations.idiomatic: During the Warring States era, rival rhetorics battled, truth and falsehood were fiercely contested, and the teachings of the philosophers became tangled and chaotic.

sentences.zh: 至秦患之，乃燔滅文章，以愚黔首。

translations.literal: Qin then was troubled by this and burned and destroyed writings, to make the black-haired commoners ignorant.
translations.idiomatic: The Qin court, alarmed by this, burned texts and erased writings in order to keep the common people ignorant.

sentences.zh: 漢興，改秦之敗，大收篇籍，廣開獻書之路。

translations.literal: When Han arose, it corrected Qin’s failures, broadly collected books and opened widely the road for presenting books.
translations.idiomatic: When the Han rose, it reversed Qin’s ruinous policy, recovered books on a large scale, and opened broad channels for textual submission.

sentences.zh: 迄孝武世，書缺簡脫，禮壞樂崩，聖上喟然而稱曰：「朕甚閔焉！」

translations.literal: By Emperor Wu’s age, books had missing slips, rites were broken and music collapsed; the sagely emperor sighed and said: "I deeply pity this!"
translations.idiomatic: By Emperor Wu’s reign, texts were fragmentary, ritual had decayed, and music had fallen apart. The emperor sighed: "I grieve deeply over this."

sentences.zh: 於是建藏書之策，置寫書之官，下及諸子傳說，皆充祕府。

translations.literal: Thereupon he established the policy of storing books and set officials for copying books; down to the masters’ transmitted sayings, all were filled into the secret archives.
translations.idiomatic: He then established a state program for book preservation and created copying offices; even works of the various masters and their transmitted sayings were gathered into the imperial archives.

sentences.zh: 至成帝時，以書頗散亡，使謁者陳農求遺書於天下。

translations.literal: By Emperor Cheng’s time, because books were much scattered and lost, he ordered Court Gentleman Chen Nong to seek remaining books throughout the empire.
translations.idiomatic: By Emperor Cheng’s reign, many texts had again been dispersed or lost, so he ordered the court envoy Chen Nong to search the realm for surviving copies.

sentences.zh: 詔光祿大夫劉向校經傳諸子詩賦，步兵校尉任宏校兵書，太史令尹咸校數術，侍醫李柱國校方技。

translations.literal: Edict: Grand Master Liu Xiang collate Classics, commentaries, masters, and fu; Infantry Commandant Ren Hong collate military books; Grand Astrologer Yin Xian collate numerological arts; Attending Physician Li Zhuguo collate technical recipes.
translations.idiomatic: An edict assigned responsibilities: Liu Xiang to collate the Classics, commentaries, philosophical works, and rhapsodies; Ren Hong to military texts; Yin Xian to numerological arts; and Li Zhuguo to medical and technical treatises.

sentences.zh: 每一書已，向輒條其篇目，撮其指意，錄而奏之。

translations.literal: Whenever one book was completed, Xiang would itemize its chapter headings, extract its main purport, record it, and memorialize it.
translations.idiomatic: Each time a text was finished, Liu Xiang listed its chapter structure, summarized its central intent, and submitted the record to the throne.

sentences.zh: 會向卒，哀帝復使向子侍中奉車都尉歆卒父業。

translations.literal: When Xiang died, Emperor Ai again ordered Xiang’s son, Attendant at Court and Commandant of Imperial Carriages, Xin, to complete his father’s work.
translations.idiomatic: After Liu Xiang died, Emperor Ai ordered his son Liu Xin, then an Attendant and commandant of the imperial carriages, to complete his father’s project.

sentences.zh: 歆於是總群書而奏其七略，故有輯略，有六藝略，有諸子略，有詩賦略，有兵書略，有術數略，有方技略。

translations.literal: Xin then gathered all books and presented his Seven Summaries; therefore there was Ji lüe, Six Arts lüe, Masters lüe, Odes and Fu lüe, Military Books lüe, Numerological Arts lüe, and Formula Techniques lüe.
translations.idiomatic: Liu Xin then systematized the corpus and submitted the Seven Summaries: the General Summary, Six Arts Summary, Masters Summary, Poetry and Rhapsody Summary, Military Books Summary, Numerological Arts Summary, and Formula/Technical Arts Summary.

translations.literal: Now I delete to its essentials, to prepare this catalog of books.
translations.idiomatic: Here I present an abridged essential version for use as a bibliographic catalog.

sentences.zh: 易經十二篇，施、孟、梁丘三家。

translations.literal: Book of Changes, 12 pian: the three schools of Shi, Meng, and Liangqiu.
translations.idiomatic: Book of Changes: 12 fascicles, in the three traditions of Shi, Meng, and Liangqiu.

translations.literal: Commentary to the Changes, Zhou lineage: 2 pian.
translations.idiomatic: Zhou-school Commentary on the Changes: 2 fascicles.

translations.literal: Fu lineage: 2 pian.
Yang lineage: 2 pian.
Master Cai: 2 pian.
Han lineage: 2 pian.
Wang lineage: 2 pian.
Ding lineage: 8 pian.
Ancient Five Masters: 18 pian.
Huainan Dao Explanations: 2 pian.

sentences.zh: 古雜八十篇，雜災異三十五篇，神輸五篇，圖一。

translations.literal: Ancient miscellany: 80 pian; Miscellaneous Portents: 35 pian; Divine Transmission: 5 pian; Charts: 1.
translations.idiomatic: Ancient miscellaneous corpus: 80 fascicles; Miscellaneous Anomaly Records: 35; Divine Transmission texts: 5; Charts: 1.

sentences.zh: 孟氏京房十一篇，災異孟氏京房六十六篇，五鹿充宗略說三篇，京氏段嘉十二篇。

translations.literal: Meng lineage Jing Fang: 11 pian; Portents Meng Jing Fang: 66 pian; Wulu Chongzong Abridged Explanations: 3 pian; Jing lineage Duan Jia: 12 pian.
translations.idiomatic: Meng-school Jing Fang texts: 11 fascicles; Meng/Jing Fang anomaly texts: 66; Wulu Chongzong’s abridged exposition: 3; Jing-school Duan Jia: 12.

sentences.zh: 章句施、孟、梁丘氏各二篇。

translations.literal: Section-and-phrase commentaries, Shi, Meng, and Liangqiu lineages each 2 pian.
translations.idiomatic: Phrase-by-phrase commentaries: Shi, Meng, and Liangqiu schools each with 2 fascicles.

sentences.zh: 凡易十三家，二百九十四篇。

translations.literal: In total, Changes texts: 13 families, 294 pian.
translations.idiomatic: Total for Changes literature: 13 lineages, 294 fascicles.

sentences.zh: 《易》曰：「宓戲氏仰觀象於天，俯觀法於地，觀鳥獸之文，與地之宜，近取諸身，遠取諸物，於是始作八卦，以通神明之德，以類萬物之情。」

translations.literal: The Changes says: "Fu Xi looked up and observed images in Heaven, looked down and observed models on Earth, observed markings of birds and beasts and what suited the land, took near from the body and far from things; thus he first made the Eight Trigrams, to communicate the virtue of spirit clarity and classify the dispositions of myriad things."
translations.idiomatic: The Changes says: "Fu Xi looked up to read heavenly patterns and down to read earthly forms; he studied the markings of birds and beasts and the fitness of terrain, taking analogies from the body nearby and from things afar. He then created the Eight Trigrams to connect with numinous insight and to categorize the nature of all things."

sentences.zh: 至於殷、周之際，紂在上位，逆天暴物，文王以諸侯順命而行道，天人之占可得而效，於是重易六爻，作上下篇。

translations.literal: By the Yin-Zhou transition, Zhou held the upper position and acted against Heaven and violently toward beings. King Wen, as a feudal lord, followed mandate and practiced the Way; divinations of Heaven and humans could be modeled, and so he doubled the Changes’ six lines and made upper and lower pian.
translations.idiomatic: At the end of Yin and beginning of Zhou, when King Zhou sat on the throne and defied Heaven while tyrannizing the people, King Wen as a regional lord upheld the mandate and practiced the Way. Since heavenly and human divination had become demonstrable, he expanded the hexagram system and produced Upper and Lower Books.

sentences.zh: 孔氏為之彖、象、繫辭、文言、序卦之屬十篇。

translations.literal: The Kong school then made ten pian of Tuan, Xiang, Xici, Wenyan, Xugua and related texts.
translations.idiomatic: The Confucian tradition then produced ten associated treatises, including the Tuan, Xiang, Xici, Wenyan, and Xugua.

sentences.zh: 故曰易道深矣，人更三聖，世歷三古。

translations.literal: Therefore it is said: the Way of the Changes is profound; through three sages and across three ancient ages.
translations.idiomatic: Hence the saying: the Way of the Changes is profoundly deep, shaped by three sages across three high antiquities.

sentences.zh: 及秦燔書，而易為筮卜之事，傳者不絕。

translations.literal: When Qin burned books, the Changes, being a matter of divination, had unbroken transmission.
translations.idiomatic: When the Qin burned books, the Changes survived because it remained in use for divination.

translations.literal: When Han arose, Tian He transmitted it.
translations.idiomatic: After the Han founding, Tian He transmitted this tradition.

sentences.zh: 訖于宣、元，有施、孟、梁丘、京氏列於學官，而民間有費、高二家之說。

translations.literal: Down to Xuandi and Yuandi, Shi, Meng, Liangqiu, and Jing lineages were listed in official learning offices, while among the people there were sayings of Fei and Gao families.
translations.idiomatic: By the reigns of Emperors Xuan and Yuan, the Shi, Meng, Liangqiu, and Jing traditions were canonized in state academies, while the Fei and Gao schools circulated privately.

sentences.zh: 劉向以中古文易經校施、孟、梁丘經，或脫去「無咎」、「悔亡」，唯費氏經與古文同。

translations.literal: Liu Xiang used a middle ancient script Changes to collate Shi, Meng, and Liangqiu texts; some had dropped "wu jiu" and "hui wang"; only Fei lineage text matched ancient script.
translations.idiomatic: Comparing with an ancient-script Changes text, Liu Xiang found that Shi, Meng, and Liangqiu versions sometimes omitted phrases such as "no blame" and "regret vanishes"; only the Fei text agreed with the ancient-script form.

translations.literal: Ancient-script Documents Classic: 46 juan.
Classic: 29 juan.
Commentaries: 41 pian.
Ouyang chapter-and-phrase commentary: 31 juan.

sentences.zh: 大、小夏侯章句各二十九卷。

translations.literal: Greater and Lesser Xiahou chapter-and-phrase commentaries each 29 juan.
translations.idiomatic: Greater Xiahou and Lesser Xiahou phrase commentaries: 29 scrolls each.

sentences.zh: 大、小夏侯解故二十九篇。

translations.literal: Greater and Lesser Xiahou explanatory old interpretations: 29 pian.
translations.idiomatic: Greater and Lesser Xiahou explanatory traditions: 29 fascicles.

translations.literal: Ouyang explanatory meanings: 2 pian.
Liu Xiang Five Phases Transmission Records: 11 juan.
translations.idiomatic: Liu Xiang’s Five-Phases transmission records: 11 scrolls.

translations.literal: Xu Shang Five Phases Transmission Records: 1 pian.
translations.idiomatic: Xu Shang’s Five-Phases transmission record: 1 fascicle.

translations.literal: Zhou Documents: 71 pian.
Deliberative memorials: 42 pian.

sentences.zh: 凡書九家，四百一十二篇。

translations.literal: In total Documents texts: 9 families, 412 pian.
translations.idiomatic: Total for Documents literature: 9 lineages, 412 fascicles.

sentences.zh: 《易》曰：「河出圖，雒出書，聖人則之。」

translations.literal: The Changes says: "The River produced the Chart; the Luo produced the Writing; sages modeled themselves on them."
translations.idiomatic: The Changes says: "The Yellow River produced the Chart, the Luo River produced the Writing, and sages took these as models."

sentences.zh: 故書之所起遠矣，至孔子篹焉，上斷於堯，下訖于秦，凡百篇，而為之序，言其作意。

translations.literal: Thus the origin of Documents is very remote. By the time of Confucius’ compilation, he cut above at Yao and below ended at Qin, in all 100 pian, and made a preface, stating the intent of each composition.
translations.idiomatic: So the origins of the Documents tradition are ancient indeed. Confucius later compiled it, beginning with Yao and ending with Qin, totaling one hundred fascicles, and wrote a preface explaining each text’s purpose.

sentences.zh: 秦燔書禁學，濟南伏生獨壁藏之。

translations.literal: When Qin burned books and banned learning, Fu Sheng of Jinan alone hid them in his wall.
translations.idiomatic: When Qin banned learning and burned texts, Fu Sheng of Jinan preserved this corpus by hiding it inside a wall.

sentences.zh: 漢興亡失，求得二十九篇，以教齊魯之間。

translations.literal: After Han arose, much had been lost; 29 pian were recovered and used to teach in Qi and Lu regions.
translations.idiomatic: After the Han founding, much had disappeared; twenty-nine fascicles were recovered and taught in the Qi-Lu region.

sentences.zh: 訖孝宣世，有歐陽、大小夏侯氏，立於學官。

translations.literal: Down to Xiaoxuan’s age, there were Ouyang and Greater/Lesser Xiahou schools established in official learning.
translations.idiomatic: By Emperor Xuan’s era, the Ouyang and the Greater/Lesser Xiahou schools had been installed in state academic offices.

sentences.zh: 古文尚書者，出孔子壁中。

translations.literal: Ancient-script Documents came out from Confucius’ wall.
translations.idiomatic: The ancient-script Documents tradition emerged from texts found in Confucius’ wall.

sentences.zh: 武帝末，魯共王壞孔子宅，欲以廣其宮，而得古文尚書及禮記、論語、孝經凡數十篇，皆古字也。

translations.literal: At the end of Emperor Wu’s reign, King Gong of Lu demolished Confucius’ house, wishing to expand his palace, and obtained ancient-script Documents, Rites Records, Analects, and Classic of Filial Piety, altogether several tens of pian, all in ancient characters.
translations.idiomatic: Late in Emperor Wu’s reign, King Gong of Lu, while attempting to enlarge his palace by dismantling Confucius’ old residence, discovered dozens of ancient-character texts, including the ancient-script Documents, Records of Rites, Analects, and Classic of Filial Piety.

sentences.zh: 共王往入其宅，聞鼓琴瑟鍾磬之音，於是懼，乃止不壞。

translations.literal: When King Gong entered the house, he heard the sounds of drums, zithers, bells, and chime stones; he was frightened and stopped demolishing it.
translations.idiomatic: When he entered the house, he heard ritual music - drums, zithers, bells, and stone chimes - and, frightened, halted the demolition.

sentences.zh: 孔安國者，孔子後也，悉得其書，以考二十九篇，得多十六篇。

translations.literal: Kong Anguo, a descendant of Confucius, obtained all those books and compared with the 29 pian, finding 16 additional pian.
translations.idiomatic: Kong Anguo, a descendant of Confucius, received the full cache and, when comparing it to the twenty-nine-fascicle corpus, identified sixteen additional fascicles.

translations.literal: Anguo presented them.
translations.idiomatic: Kong Anguo submitted these texts to the court.

translations.literal: But because of the witchcraft affair, they were not listed in official learning.
translations.idiomatic: Because of the later witchcraft prosecutions, however, they were never incorporated into the official curriculum.

sentences.zh: 劉向以中古文校歐陽、大小夏侯三家經文，酒誥脫簡一，召誥脫簡二。

translations.literal: Liu Xiang used middle ancient script to collate the Ouyang and Greater/Lesser Xiahou three-school texts: in "Wine Proclamation" one missing slip, in "Shao Proclamation" two missing slips.
translations.idiomatic: Using an ancient-script witness, Liu Xiang collated the three schools (Ouyang, Greater Xiahou, Lesser Xiahou), finding one missing slip in the "Wine Proclamation" and two in the "Shao Proclamation."

sentences.zh: 率簡二十五字者，脫亦二十五字，簡二十二字者，脫亦二十二字，文字異者七百有餘，脫字數十。

translations.literal: Generally, if a slip had 25 characters, the omission was likewise 25; if 22, omission likewise 22. There were over 700 variant characters and dozens of omitted characters.
translations.idiomatic: Typically, omissions matched whole-slip length: 25-character slips omitted by 25, 22-character slips by 22. He also recorded over 700 graph variants and dozens of omitted characters.

sentences.zh: 書者，古之號令，號令於眾，其言不立具，則聽受施行者弗曉。

translations.literal: Documents are the ancient proclamations and commands. When commanding the many, if wording is not fully established, those receiving and executing cannot understand.
translations.idiomatic: Documents were ancient proclamations and state commands. If wording was not fully fixed, those tasked to receive and implement them could not understand correctly.

sentences.zh: 古文讀應爾雅，故解古今語而可知也。

translations.literal: Ancient-script readings accord with Erya; therefore explaining ancient and modern words makes them knowable.
translations.idiomatic: Ancient-script readings align with the Erya lexicon; thus, by glossing ancient and current diction, the texts become intelligible.

sentences.zh: 詩經二十八卷，魯、齊、韓三家。

translations.literal: Classic of Odes: 28 juan, the three schools of Lu, Qi, and Han.
translations.idiomatic: Classic of Odes: 28 scrolls, in the three traditions of Lu, Qi, and Han.

translations.literal: Lu old explanations: 25 juan.
Lu explanations: 28 juan.
Qi Hou lineage old explanations: 20 juan.
Qi Sun lineage old explanations: 27 juan.
Qi Hou lineage transmission: 39 juan.
Qi Sun lineage transmission: 28 juan.
Qi miscellany notes: 18 juan.
Han old explanations: 36 juan.
Han inner transmission: 4 juan.
Han outer transmission: 6 juan.
Han explanations: 41 juan.
Mao Odes: 29 juan.
translations.idiomatic: Mao recension of the Odes: 29 scrolls.

translations.literal: Mao Odes old gloss-transmission: 30 juan.
translations.idiomatic: Mao Odes old gloss-and-transmission text: 30 scrolls.

sentences.zh: 凡詩六家，四百一十六卷。

translations.literal: In total Odes: 6 families, 416 juan.
The Documents says: "Poetry speaks intent; song chants words."
translations.idiomatic: A classic line says: "Poetry expresses intent; song voices those words."

sentences.zh: 故哀樂之心感，而哥詠之聲發。

translations.literal: Therefore when the heart of sorrow and joy is moved, the sounds of song and chant issue forth.
translations.idiomatic: Thus when the heart is stirred by grief or joy, chant and melody arise.

sentences.zh: 誦其言謂之詩，詠其聲謂之哥。

translations.literal: Reciting its words is called poetry; chanting its sound is called song.
translations.idiomatic: When its language is recited, it is called poetry; when its sound is intoned, it is called song.

sentences.zh: 故古有采詩之官，王者所以觀風俗，知得失，自考正也。

translations.literal: Hence in antiquity there were officials for collecting poems; this is how kings observed customs, knew gains and losses, and examined and corrected themselves.
translations.idiomatic: Accordingly, antiquity had officials who collected poems, allowing rulers to observe regional customs, assess political success and failure, and correct themselves.

sentences.zh: 孔子純取周詩，上采殷，下取魯，凡三百五篇，遭秦而全者，以其諷誦，不獨在竹帛故也。

translations.literal: Confucius purely selected Zhou poems, taking above from Yin and below from Lu, in all 305 pieces. Those that survived Qin did so because of oral chanting, not solely because they were on bamboo and silk.
translations.idiomatic: Confucius chiefly selected Zhou poems, while also drawing from Yin above and Lu below, totaling 305 pieces. What survived the Qin proscription survived through oral recitation, not merely written copies.

sentences.zh: 漢興，魯申公為詩訓故，而齊轅固、燕韓生皆為之傳。

translations.literal: When Han arose, Shen Gong of Lu made Odes exegesis, and Yuan Gu of Qi and Han Sheng of Yan also made transmissions.
translations.idiomatic: After the Han founding, Shen Gong of Lu established an exegetical tradition on the Odes, and Yuan Gu of Qi and Han Sheng of Yan each transmitted their own lines.

sentences.zh: 或取春秋，采雜說，咸非其本義。

translations.literal: Some borrowed from Spring and Autumn and gathered miscellaneous sayings, all not the original meaning.
translations.idiomatic: Some interpreters imported Spring and Autumn methods and miscellaneous theories, often diverging from the original meaning.

translations.literal: Among what could be accepted under necessity, Lu was closest to it.
translations.idiomatic: Of the available traditions, the Lu line remained closest to the intended sense.

translations.literal: All three schools were listed in official learning offices.
translations.idiomatic: All three schools were institutionalized in state education.

sentences.zh: 又有毛公之學，自謂子夏所傳，而河間獻王好之，未得立。

translations.literal: There was also Master Mao’s learning, claiming transmission from Zixia; the King Xian of Hejian favored it, yet it was not established.
translations.idiomatic: There was also the Mao tradition, which claimed descent from Zixia’s transmission. Though King Xian of Hejian esteemed it, it was not officially installed.

sentences.zh: 禮古經五十六卷，經七十篇。

translations.literal: Ancient Classic of Rites: 56 juan; Classics 70 pian.
translations.idiomatic: Ancient Rites corpus: 56 scrolls; canonical component: 70 fascicles.

translations.literal: Records: 131 pian.
Mingtang Yin-Yang: 33 pian.
Wang and Shi lineage texts: 21 pian.
Qutai Hou Cang: 9 pian.
translations.idiomatic: Qutai recension of Hou Cang: 9 fascicles.

translations.literal: Explanations of the Doctrine of the Mean: 2 pian.
translations.idiomatic: Interpretations of the Doctrine of the Mean: 2 fascicles.

translations.literal: Mingtang Yin-Yang explanations: 5 pian.
Zhou Offices Classic: 6 pian.
Zhou Offices transmissions: 4 pian.

sentences.zh: 軍禮司馬法百五十五篇。

translations.literal: Military rites, Sima Methods: 155 pian.
Ancient Feng and Shan plus group sacrifices: 22 pian.
translations.idiomatic: Ancient Feng-Shan and collective sacrifice texts: 22 fascicles.

translations.literal: Feng and Shan deliberative responses: 19 pian.
translations.idiomatic: Feng-Shan debate and response texts: 19 fascicles.

translations.literal: Han Feng and Shan plus group sacrifices: 36 pian.
translations.idiomatic: Han-era Feng-Shan and collective sacrifice texts: 36 fascicles.

translations.literal: Deliberative memorials: 38 pian.

sentences.zh: 凡禮十三家，五百五十五篇。

translations.literal: In total Rites: 13 families, 555 pian.
translations.idiomatic: Total for Rites literature: 13 lineages, 555 fascicles.

sentences.zh: 《易》曰：「有夫婦父子君臣上下，禮義有所錯。」

translations.literal: The Changes says: "There are husband-wife, father-son, ruler-minister, above-below; ritual propriety has where to be arranged."
translations.idiomatic: The Changes says: "With husband and wife, father and son, ruler and minister, superior and subordinate, ritual principle has its proper ordering."

sentences.zh: 而帝王質文世有損益，至周曲為之防，事為之制，故曰：「禮經三百，威儀三千。」

translations.literal: As for emperors and kings, plainness and adornment in ages had reductions and additions. By Zhou they made detailed defenses and made systems for affairs; therefore it says: "Ritual classics are three hundred, dignified forms three thousand."
translations.idiomatic: Dynasties adjusted between plainness and refinement, adding and subtracting over time. By the Zhou, regulations were made highly detailed and institutions formalized. Hence the saying: "Three hundred ritual canons, three thousand ceremonial forms."

sentences.zh: 及周之衰，諸侯將踰法度，惡其害己，皆滅去其籍，自孔子時而不具，至秦大壞。

translations.literal: When Zhou declined, feudal lords were about to exceed norms and, hating restrictions to themselves, all destroyed their records. From Confucius’ time it was incomplete; by Qin it was greatly ruined.
translations.idiomatic: As Zhou power waned, feudal rulers overstepped norms and destroyed records they found inconvenient. The corpus was already incomplete in Confucius’ day, and under Qin it was devastated.

sentences.zh: 漢興，魯高堂生傳士禮十七篇。

translations.literal: When Han arose, Gaotang Sheng of Lu transmitted 17 pian of Scholar Rites.
translations.idiomatic: After the Han founding, Gaotang Sheng of Lu transmitted a 17-fascicle "Scholar Rites" tradition.

translations.literal: By Emperor Xuan’s age, Hou Cang was the clearest expert.
translations.idiomatic: By Emperor Xuan’s reign, Hou Cang was the foremost authority.

sentences.zh: 戴德、戴聖、慶普皆其弟子，三家立於學官。

translations.literal: Dai De, Dai Sheng, and Qing Pu were all his disciples; the three families were established in official learning.
translations.idiomatic: Dai De, Dai Sheng, and Qing Pu were all his students, and their three lineages were installed in state scholarship.

sentences.zh: 禮古經者，出於魯淹中及孔氏，學七十篇文相似，多三十九篇。

translations.literal: Ancient Rites Classics came from Lu’s Yanzhong and the Kong lineage. Its study had 70 pian similar in wording, with 39 additional pian.
translations.idiomatic: The ancient-script Rites texts came from Yanzhong in Lu and the Kong family holdings. This tradition had 70 core fascicles with similar wording, plus 39 additional fascicles.

sentences.zh: 及明堂陰陽、王史氏記所見，多天子諸侯卿大夫之制，雖不能備，猶瘉倉等推士禮而致於天子之說。

translations.literal: As for Mingtang Yin-Yang and Wang-Shi records, what is seen includes many systems of Son of Heaven, feudal lords, and high officers. Though not complete, it still exceeds Cang and others, who extrapolated from Scholar Rites up to Son-of-Heaven theory.
translations.idiomatic: The Mingtang Yin-Yang and Wang-Shi materials preserve many institutional details for emperor, feudal lords, and ministers. Though incomplete, they remain stronger than attempts to extrapolate imperial ritual from "Scholar Rites" alone.

translations.literal: Record of Music: 23 pian.
Record of Music: 23 pian.
Wang Yu Records: 24 pian.
Elegant Song Odes: 4 pian.
Elegant qin, Zhao lineage: 7 pian.
translations.idiomatic: Elegant zither tradition, Zhao school: 7 fascicles.

translations.literal: Elegant qin, Shi lineage: 8 pian.
translations.idiomatic: Elegant zither tradition, Shi school: 8 fascicles.

translations.literal: Elegant qin, Long lineage: 99 pian.
translations.idiomatic: Elegant zither tradition, Long school: 99 fascicles.

translations.literal: In total Music: 6 families, 165 pian.
translations.idiomatic: Total for Music literature: 6 lineages, 165 fascicles.

sentences.zh: 《易》曰：「先王作樂崇德，殷薦之上帝，以享祖考。」

translations.literal: The Changes says: "Former kings made music to exalt virtue, richly presenting to the High Thearch, thereby honoring ancestors."
translations.idiomatic: The Changes says: "Former kings created music to exalt virtue, offering it up to the High Thearch and thereby honoring their ancestors."

sentences.zh: 故自黃帝下至三代，樂各有名。

translations.literal: Thus from Yellow Emperor down to the Three Dynasties, each music had its own name.
translations.idiomatic: So from the Yellow Emperor through the Three Dynasties, each age had its own named musical system.

sentences.zh: 孔子曰：「安上治民，莫善於禮；

translations.literal: Confucius said: "To settle superiors and govern the people, nothing is better than ritual;
translations.idiomatic: Confucius said: "For stabilizing political order, nothing surpasses ritual.

translations.literal: To transform customs, nothing is better than music."
translations.idiomatic: For transforming social custom, nothing surpasses music."

translations.literal: The two proceed together side by side.
translations.idiomatic: These two must operate in tandem.

sentences.zh: 周衰俱壞，樂尤微眇，以音律為節，又為鄭衛所亂故無遺法。

translations.literal: When Zhou declined both collapsed; music especially became subtle and obscure. Since pitch and measure were its regulation, and was further disordered by Zheng and Wey influences, no surviving method remained.
translations.idiomatic: When the Zhou order collapsed, both ritual and music decayed, with music especially becoming faint and opaque. Its tonal system was disrupted by Zheng and Wey styles, leaving no intact standard method.

sentences.zh: 漢興，制氏以雅樂聲律，世在樂官，頗能紀其鏗鏘鼓舞，而不能言其義。

translations.literal: After Han arose, the Zhi lineage, with elegant music tones and pitch-laws, for generations served in music offices and could somewhat record clangor and dances, but could not explain their meaning.
translations.idiomatic: After Han was founded, the Zhi lineage preserved court tonal practice and served for generations in music offices. They could reproduce performance forms but could not articulate their principles.

sentences.zh: 六國之君，魏文侯最為好古，孝文時得其樂人竇公，獻其書，乃周官大宗伯之大司樂章也。

translations.literal: Among rulers of the Six States, Marquis Wen of Wei most loved antiquity. In Emperor Wen’s time they obtained his musician Dou Gong, who offered his book, namely the chapter of Great Director of Music in Zhou Officials Great Minister of Rites.
translations.idiomatic: Of the Warring States rulers, Marquis Wen of Wei had most cherished antiquity. In Emperor Wen’s reign, his musician Dou Gong was obtained and submitted a text identified with the Great Director of Music section under the Grand Minister of Rites in the Zhou Offices.

sentences.zh: 武帝時，河間獻王好儒，與毛生等共采周官及諸子言樂事者，以作樂記，獻八佾之舞，與制氏不相遠。

translations.literal: In Emperor Wu’s time, King Xian of Hejian loved Ru learning and with Mao Sheng and others collected Zhou Offices and all masters’ remarks on music to make Record of Music, presenting Eight-Row Dance, not far from Zhi lineage.
translations.idiomatic: Under Emperor Wu, King Xian of Hejian, a patron of classical learning, worked with Mao Sheng and others to compile materials on music from the Zhou Offices and other masters into a Record of Music, and presented an Eight-Rank Dance tradition close to that of the Zhi school.

sentences.zh: 其內史丞王定傳之，以授常山王禹。

translations.literal: His Assistant Director Wang Ding transmitted it and taught it to Wang Yu of Changshan.
translations.idiomatic: His assistant director Wang Ding transmitted this line and taught it to Wang Yu of Changshan.

sentences.zh: 禹，成帝時為謁者，數言其義，獻二十四卷記。

translations.literal: Yu, in Emperor Cheng’s time as Court Gentleman, repeatedly explained its meaning and presented a 24-juan record.
translations.idiomatic: Wang Yu later served as a court envoy under Emperor Cheng, repeatedly expounding its doctrine and submitting a 24-scroll record.

sentences.zh: 劉向校書，得樂記二十三篇，與禹不同，其道浸以益微。

translations.literal: When Liu Xiang collated texts, he obtained a 23-pian Record of Music, differing from Yu; its way gradually became ever more faint.
translations.idiomatic: When Liu Xiang performed collation, he found a 23-fascicle Record of Music text that differed from Wang Yu’s, indicating the tradition had by then grown increasingly obscure.

sentences.zh: 春秋古經十二篇，經十一卷。

translations.literal: Ancient Spring and Autumn Classic: 12 pian; Classic 11 juan.
translations.idiomatic: Ancient Spring and Autumn Classic: 12 fascicles; canonical core: 11 scrolls.

translations.literal: Zuo Commentary: 30 juan.
Gongyang Commentary: 11 juan.
Guliang Commentary: 11 juan.
Zou lineage commentary: 11 juan.
Jia lineage commentary: 11 juan.
Zuo subtleties: 2 pian.
Duo subtleties: 3 pian.
Zhang subtleties: 10 pian.
Yu lineage subtle transmission: 2 pian.
Gongyang Outer Transmission: 50 pian.
Guliang Outer Transmission: 20 pian.
Gongyang chapter-and-phrase commentary: 38 pian.
Guliang chapter-and-phrase commentary: 33 pian.
Gongyang miscellany notes: 83 pian.
Gongyang Yan lineage records: 11 pian.

sentences.zh: 公羊董仲舒治獄十六篇。

translations.literal: Gongyang, Dong Zhongshu judicial case texts: 16 pian.
translations.idiomatic: Gongyang judicial-case writings attributed to Dong Zhongshu: 16 fascicles.

translations.literal: Deliberative memorials: 39 pian.
Discourses of the States: 21 pian.
New Discourses of the States: 54 pian.
translations.idiomatic: New Discourses of the States: 54 fascicles.

translations.literal: Shiben: 15 pian.
Strategies of the Warring States: 23 pian.
translations.idiomatic: Strategies of the Warring States: 23 fascicles.

translations.literal: Memorial Affairs: 20 pian.
Chu-Han Spring and Autumn: 9 pian.
Grand Historian: 130 pian.
Continued Grand Historian by Feng Shang: 7 pian.
translations.idiomatic: Feng Shang’s continuation of the Grand Historian: 7 fascicles.

translations.literal: Chronologies since Great Antiquity: 2 pian.
translations.idiomatic: Chronologies from high antiquity onward: 2 fascicles.

translations.literal: Han Recorded Annals: 190 juan.
Great Han Chronological Annals: 5 pian.

sentences.zh: 凡春秋二十三家，九百四十八篇。

translations.literal: In total Spring and Autumn works: 23 families, 948 pian.
translations.idiomatic: Total Spring and Autumn literature: 23 lineages, 948 fascicles.

sentences.zh: 古之王者世有史官，君舉必書，所以慎言行，昭法式也。

translations.literal: In antiquity, kings in each generation had historiographic offices; whatever ruler undertook was necessarily recorded, in order to make speech and conduct cautious and make standards manifest.
translations.idiomatic: In antiquity, every dynasty maintained historical offices, and every act of rulership was recorded, so that words and conduct would be restrained and normative models made clear.

sentences.zh: 左史記言，右史記事，事為春秋，言為尚書，帝王靡不同之。

translations.literal: The left scribe recorded words; the right scribe recorded affairs. Affairs became Spring and Autumn; words became Documents. Emperors and kings were none who did not follow this.
translations.idiomatic: The left historian recorded speech and the right historian recorded deeds; from deeds came the Spring and Autumn record, from speech the Documents corpus. No legitimate ruler was without this system.

sentences.zh: 周室既微，載籍殘缺，仲尼思存前聖之業，乃稱曰：「夏禮吾能言之，杞不足徵也；

translations.literal: After the Zhou house weakened and records became damaged and incomplete, Confucius thought to preserve the enterprise of former sages and said: "Xia rites I can speak of, but Qi is insufficient to verify them;
translations.idiomatic: When Zhou authority declined and archives became fragmentary, Confucius sought to preserve the legacy of earlier sages and said, "I can describe Xia ritual, but the state of Qi lacks evidence to confirm it.

sentences.zh: 殷禮吾能言之，宋不足徵也。

translations.literal: Yin rites I can speak of, but Song is insufficient to verify them.
translations.idiomatic: I can describe Yin ritual, but Song lacks enough evidence to verify it.

sentences.zh: 文獻不足故也，足則吾能徵之矣。」

translations.literal: Because documents and worthies are insufficient. If sufficient, then I could verify them."
translations.idiomatic: That is because documentary and learned evidence is insufficient; if it were sufficient, I could verify them fully."

sentences.zh: 以魯周公之國，禮文備物，史官有法，故與左丘明觀其史記，據行事，仍人道，因興以立功，就敗以成罰，假日月以定曆數，藉朝聘以正禮樂。

translations.literal: Because Lu was the state of Duke of Zhou, with complete ritual documents and institutions, and historiographic offices had methods, therefore with Zuo Qiuming he viewed those historical records, based on events, followed human ways, used prospering to establish merit, used failure to complete punishment, borrowed sun and moon to fix calendrical numbers, and relied on court audiences and visits to rectify rites and music.
translations.idiomatic: Because Lu - as the state of the Duke of Zhou - still preserved rich ritual culture and institutional archives, Confucius and Zuo Qiuming examined its records and, grounding themselves in concrete events and human norms, formulated moral judgments: rewarding success, assigning punishment for failure, using calendrical markers to date events, and using diplomatic ritual to set standards for rites and music.

sentences.zh: 有所褒諱貶損，不可書見，口授弟子，弟子退而異言。

translations.literal: As for praise, concealment, blame, and diminution, some could not be written openly, so he orally transmitted to disciples; when disciples withdrew they spoke differently.
translations.idiomatic: Some evaluations - praise, concealment, censure, and reduction - could not be written explicitly, so they were orally transmitted to disciples, who then repeated them divergently.

sentences.zh: 丘明恐弟子各安其意，以失其真，故論本事而作傳，明夫子不以空言說經也。

translations.literal: Qiuming feared each disciple would settle on his own meaning and lose the truth, therefore discussed the underlying events and made the Commentary, clarifying that the Master did not explain the Classic with empty words.
translations.idiomatic: Fearing that disciples would each interpret by private preference and lose the truth, Zuo Qiuming wrote commentary grounded in the facts, showing that the Master’s exegesis was never empty rhetoric.

sentences.zh: 春秋所貶損大人當世君臣，有威權勢力，其事實皆形於傳，是以隱其書而不宣，所以免時難也。

translations.literal: What the Spring and Autumn censured included great persons and contemporary rulers and ministers, those with authority and power; their factual matters all appeared in the Commentary. Therefore he concealed his book and did not publish it, thereby avoiding the difficulties of his time.
translations.idiomatic: Because the Spring and Autumn censured powerful contemporaries - rulers, ministers, and men of authority - the factual basis was made explicit in the Commentary. For this reason the text was kept hidden rather than publicly circulated, as a means of avoiding political danger.

sentences.zh: 及末世口說流行，故有公羊、穀梁、鄒、夾之傳。

translations.literal: By later ages oral teachings circulated, thus there were Gongyang, Guliang, Zou, and Jia commentarial traditions.
translations.idiomatic: In later generations, oral interpretations spread into distinct commentarial lineages, including the Gongyang, Guliang, Zou, and Jia traditions.

sentences.zh: 四家之中，公羊、穀梁立於學官，鄒氏無師，夾氏未有書。

translations.literal: Among the four families, Gongyang and Guliang were established in official learning; Zou had no teachers; Jia had no written books yet.
translations.idiomatic: Of these four, only Gongyang and Guliang entered official education; the Zou school lacked a teaching lineage, and the Jia school had no complete text.

translations.literal: Ancient Analects: 21 pian.
Qi version: 22 pian.
Lu version: 20 pian; transmission 19 pian.
translations.idiomatic: Lu recension: 20 fascicles; transmission text: 19 fascicles.

translations.literal: Qi explanations: 29 pian.
Lu Xiahou explanations: 21 pian.
Lu Angchang Marquis explanations: 21 pian.
Lu King Jun explanations: 20 pian.
Yan transmission explanations: 3 juan.
Deliberative memorials: 18 pian.
Kongzi Family Sayings: 27 juan.
Confucius at Three Courts: 7 pian.
Confucius disciples diagrams and methods: 2 juan.
translations.idiomatic: Diagrams and methods of Confucius’ disciples: 2 scrolls.

sentences.zh: 凡論語十二家，二百二十九篇。

translations.literal: In total Analects works: 12 families, 229 pian.

sentences.zh: 論語者，孔子應答弟子時人及弟子相與言而接聞於夫子之語也。

translations.literal: The Analects are responses of Confucius to disciples and people of the time, and disciples’ mutual sayings that connected to words heard from the Master.
translations.idiomatic: The Analects preserve Confucius’ replies to disciples and contemporaries, as well as disciples’ own exchanges linked to teachings heard from the Master.

translations.literal: At that time, disciples each had their own records.
translations.idiomatic: At the time, each disciple kept his own notes.

sentences.zh: 夫子既卒，門人相與輯而論篹，故謂之論語。

translations.literal: After the Master had died, the followers mutually assembled, discussed, and compiled; therefore it was called Analects.
translations.idiomatic: After the Master’s death, the disciples gathered these records, edited and arranged them, and thus the work came to be called the Analects.

translations.literal: When Han arose, there were Qi and Lu explanations.
translations.idiomatic: With the Han founding, both Qi and Lu interpretive traditions circulated.

sentences.zh: 傳齊論者，昌邑中尉王吉、少府宋畸、御史大夫貢禹、尚書令五鹿充宗、膠東庸生，唯王陽名家。

translations.literal: Those transmitting Qi Analects: Wang Ji, commandant of Changyi; Song Ji, Lesser Treasury; Gong Yu, Censor-in-chief; Wulu Chongzong, Director of the Secretariat; and Yong Sheng of Jiaodong. Only Wang Yang was an eminent lineage.
translations.idiomatic: Transmitters of the Qi Analects included Wang Ji of Changyi, Song Ji of the Lesser Treasury, Gong Yu the Censor-in-chief, Wulu Chongzong the Secretariat Director, and Yong Sheng of Jiaodong; among them, Wang Yang was especially renowned.

sentences.zh: 傳魯論語者，常山都尉龔奮、長信少府夏侯勝、丞相韋賢、魯扶卿、前將軍蕭望之、安昌侯張禹，皆名家。

translations.literal: Those transmitting Lu Analects: Gong Fen, commandant of Changshan; Xiahou Sheng, Lesser Treasury of Changxin; Chancellor Wei Xian; Lu Fuqing; Former General Xiao Wangzhi; and Marquis Angchang Zhang Yu, all eminent lineages.
translations.idiomatic: Transmitters of the Lu Analects included Gong Fen of Changshan, Xiahou Sheng of Changxin, Chancellor Wei Xian, Lu Fuqing, Former General Xiao Wangzhi, and Marquis Zhang Yu of Angchang - all noted authorities.

translations.literal: The Zhang lineage was latest and circulated in the world.
translations.idiomatic: The Zhang recension emerged latest and became the one most widely used.

translations.literal: Classic of Filial Piety ancient Kong lineage: 1 pian.
translations.idiomatic: Classic of Filial Piety, ancient Kong recension: 1 fascicle.

translations.literal: Classic of Filial Piety ancient Kong lineage: 1 pian.
translations.idiomatic: Classic of Filial Piety, ancient Kong recension: 1 fascicle.

translations.literal: Classic of Filial Piety: 1 pian.
translations.idiomatic: Classic of Filial Piety standard text: 1 fascicle.

translations.literal: Changsun lineage explanations: 2 pian.
Jiang lineage explanations: 1 pian.
Yi lineage explanations: 1 pian.
Hou lineage explanations: 1 pian.
Miscellaneous transmissions: 4 pian.
Angchang Marquis explanations: 1 pian.
Miscellaneous discussions on the Five Classics: 18 pian.
Erya: 3 juan, 20 pian.

sentences.zh: 小爾雅一篇，古今字一卷。

translations.literal: Lesser Erya: 1 pian; Ancient and Modern Graphs: 1 juan.
translations.idiomatic: Lesser Erya: 1 fascicle; Ancient-and-Modern Graph forms: 1 scroll.

translations.literal: Disciple Duties: 1 pian.
Explanations: 3 pian.

sentences.zh: 凡孝經十一家，五十九篇。

translations.literal: In total Classic of Filial Piety: 11 families, 59 pian.
translations.idiomatic: Total Filial Piety literature: 11 lineages, 59 fascicles.

sentences.zh: 孝經者，孔子為曾子陳孝道也。

translations.literal: The Classic of Filial Piety is Confucius expounding the Way of filiality for Zengzi.
translations.idiomatic: The Classic of Filial Piety records Confucius explaining filial principle to Zengzi.

sentences.zh: 夫孝，天之經，地之義，民之行也。

translations.literal: Filiality is Heaven’s constant, Earth’s rightness, and the people’s conduct.
translations.idiomatic: Filiality is the norm of Heaven, the moral principle of Earth, and the basic practice of human society.

translations.literal: It states the greater points, therefore it is called the Classic of Filiality.
translations.idiomatic: Because it articulates the highest essentials, it is titled the Classic of Filial Piety.

sentences.zh: 漢興，長孫氏、博士江翁、少府后倉、諫大夫翼奉、安昌侯張禹傳之，各自名家。

translations.literal: When Han arose, Changsun lineage, Academician Jiang Weng, Lesser Treasury Hou Cang, Remonstrance Grandee Yi Feng, and Marquis Angchang Zhang Yu transmitted it, each becoming a named school.
translations.idiomatic: After the Han founding, it was transmitted by the Changsun line, Academician Jiang Weng, Hou Cang of the Lesser Treasury, Remonstrance Grandee Yi Feng, and Marquis Zhang Yu of Angchang, each forming a recognized school.

sentences.zh: 經文皆同，唯孔氏壁中古文為異。

translations.literal: The classic text is the same in all, only the ancient-script text from Kong lineage wall is different.
translations.idiomatic: Their base text was generally the same; only the ancient-script version from the Kong wall cache differed.

sentences.zh: 「父母生之，續莫大焉」，「故親生之膝下」，諸家說不安處，古文字讀皆異。

translations.literal: In phrases like "Parents gave birth to one; continuation has nothing greater" and "thus one is born under parents’ knees," places where schools’ explanations were unstable have all different readings in ancient script.
translations.idiomatic: For lines such as "Parents gave us life; no duty surpasses continuing the line" and "one is raised at one’s parents’ knees," the received schools disagree, while the ancient-script readings diverge throughout.

translations.literal: Shi Zhou: 15 pian.
Eight scripts and six techniques.
translations.idiomatic: Eight script forms and six technical methods.

translations.literal: Cangjie: 1 pian.
Fangjiang: 1 pian.
Jijiu: 1 pian.
Yuanshang: 1 pian.
Xunzuan: 1 pian.
Separate graphs: 13 pian.
Cangjie transmission: 1 pian.
Yang Xiong Cangjie Xunzuan: 1 pian.
Du Lin Cangjie Xunzuan: 1 pian.
Du Lin Cangjie old interpretation: 1 pian.
translations.idiomatic: Du Lin’s old Cangjie exegesis: 1 fascicle.

translations.literal: In total elementary studies: 10 families, 45 pian.

sentences.zh: 《易》曰：「上古結繩以治，後世聖人易之以書契，百官以治，萬民以察，蓋取諸夬。」

translations.literal: The Changes says: "In high antiquity they governed by knotted cords; later sages changed this to written contracts. Hundred officials governed by it, myriad people examined by it, likely taken from Guai."
translations.idiomatic: The Changes says: "In remote antiquity, governance relied on knotted cords; later sages replaced this with writing and tallied documents. Officials governed through it, and the people could verify through it - this is likely what the Guai hexagram signifies."

sentences.zh: 「夬，揚於王庭」，言其宣揚於王者朝廷，其用最大也。

translations.literal: "Guai is proclaimed in the royal court" means it is announced in the king’s court; its use is greatest.
translations.idiomatic: The line "Guai is proclaimed in the royal court" means public promulgation before royal authority; its political function is supreme.

sentences.zh: 古者八歲入小學，故周官保氏掌養國子，教之六書，謂象形、象事、象意、象聲、轉注、假借，造字之本也。

translations.literal: In antiquity at age eight one entered elementary school; thus in Zhou Offices the tutor-in-chief nourished state sons and taught them the six scripts: pictograph, event-graph, idea-graph, sound-graph, derivative annotation, phonetic loan - these are roots of character-making.
translations.idiomatic: In antiquity children entered elementary study at eight. Thus the Zhou Offices say the royal tutor taught the six writing principles: pictographs, indicatives, associative compounds, phonetic compounds, derivative cognates, and phonetic loans - the foundations of character formation.

sentences.zh: 漢興，蕭何草律，亦著其法，曰：「太史試學童，能諷書九千字以上，乃得為史。

translations.literal: When Han arose, Xiao He drafted laws and also recorded this rule: "When the Grand Astrologer tests schoolboys, only if they can recite over 9,000 characters may they become scribes.
translations.idiomatic: After Han was founded, Xiao He’s legal code included this provision: "In examinations by the Grand Astrologer, schoolboys who can recite over 9,000 graphs may serve as clerks.

sentences.zh: 又以六體試之，課最者以為尚書御史史書令史。

translations.literal: Again test them by six scripts; those with highest grades are made secretariat and censor clerks and document clerks.
translations.idiomatic: They are further tested in the six scripts, and the top performers are appointed to secretariat and censorial clerical posts.

sentences.zh: 吏民上書，字或不正，輒舉劾。」

translations.literal: When officials and people submit memorials, if characters are incorrect they are immediately impeached."
translations.idiomatic: If officials or commoners submit petitions with incorrect characters, charges are immediately brought."

sentences.zh: 六體者，古文、奇字、篆書、隸書、繆篆、蟲書，皆所以通知古今文字，摹印章，書幡信也。

translations.literal: The six scripts are ancient script, strange script, seal script, clerical script, twisted seal, and insect script - all for communicating ancient and modern writing, carving seals, and writing banners and credentials.
translations.idiomatic: These six script forms - ancient script, odd script, seal script, clerical script, decorative seal script, and worm script - served to bridge ancient and current writing, engrave seals, and write pennants and credentials.

sentences.zh: 古制，書必同文，不知則闕，問諸故老，至於衰世，是非無正，人用其私。

translations.literal: In ancient system writing had to share one script; if unknown one left it blank and asked elders. By declining ages, right and wrong had no standard and people used private preferences.
translations.idiomatic: Anciently, script standardization was required. Unknown forms were left unresolved until checked with elders. In later decline, standards collapsed and private usage prevailed.

sentences.zh: 故孔子曰：「吾猶及史之闕文也，今亡矣夫！」

translations.literal: Therefore Confucius said: "I still reached the historians’ lacunary characters; now they are gone!"
translations.idiomatic: Hence Confucius lamented: "I once still saw the historians’ unresolved characters; now even that is gone."

translations.literal: This lamented that writing was gradually becoming uncorrected.
translations.idiomatic: He mourned the progressive loss of orthographic correctness.

sentences.zh: 史籀篇者，周時史官教學童書也，與孔氏壁中古文異體。

translations.literal: The Shi Zhou pian was a Zhou-period scribal teaching text for schoolboys, in script-form different from the ancient-script text in Kong wall.
translations.idiomatic: The Shi Zhou text was a Zhou scribal primer for children, written in forms different from those in the Kong-wall ancient-script cache.

sentences.zh: 蒼頡七章者，秦丞相李斯所作也；

translations.literal: The seven chapters of Cangjie were made by Qin chancellor Li Si;
translations.idiomatic: The seven Cangjie chapters were composed by Qin Chancellor Li Si;

sentences.zh: 爰歷六章者，車府令趙高所作也；

translations.literal: The six chapters of Yuanli were made by Carriage Office Director Zhao Gao;
translations.idiomatic: the six Yuanli chapters by Zhao Gao, Director of the Carriage Office;

sentences.zh: 博學七章者，太史令胡母敬所作也：文字多取史籀篇，而篆體復頗異，所謂秦篆者也。

translations.literal: The seven chapters of Boxue were made by Grand Astrologer Hu Wujing. Their characters mostly took from Shi Zhou and seal forms were again rather different - what is called Qin seal.
translations.idiomatic: and the seven Boxue chapters by Grand Astrologer Hu Wujing. Their character stock drew largely from Shi Zhou but with altered seal forms, producing what later was called Qin seal script.

sentences.zh: 是時始造隸書矣，起於官獄多事，苟趨省易，施之於徒隸也。

translations.literal: At this time clerical script was first created, arising from many affairs in offices and prisons; hastily seeking economy and ease, it was applied among convict clerks.
translations.idiomatic: It was then that clerical script first emerged, born of bureaucratic and penal workload: for speed and simplification, it was adopted by low-level clerks and penal functionaries.

sentences.zh: 漢書，閭里書師合蒼頡、爰歷、博學三篇，斷六十字以為一章，凡五十五章，并為蒼頡篇。

translations.literal: In Han books, local writing teachers combined Cangjie, Yuanli, and Boxue into one. They cut 60 characters as one chapter, in all 55 chapters, together as Cangjie pian.
translations.idiomatic: In Han times, local script teachers combined Cangjie, Yuanli, and Boxue into a single teaching text, arranging 60 graphs per chapter for 55 chapters total, collectively called the Cangjie text.

sentences.zh: 武帝時司馬相如作凡將篇，無復字。

translations.literal: In Emperor Wu’s time Sima Xiangru composed Fangjiang pian, with no repeated characters.
translations.idiomatic: Under Emperor Wu, Sima Xiangru composed the Fangjiang text using non-repeating characters.

sentences.zh: 元帝時黃門令史游作急就篇，成帝時將作大匠李長作元尚篇，皆蒼頡中正字也。

translations.literal: In Emperor Yuan’s time Palace Gate Director Shi You composed Jijiu pian; in Emperor Cheng’s time Chief Works Li Chang composed Yuanshang pian; all used proper standard characters from Cangjie.
translations.idiomatic: In Emperor Yuan’s reign, Shi You wrote the Jijiu text; in Emperor Cheng’s reign, Li Chang wrote Yuanshang. Both followed standardized Cangjie orthography.

translations.literal: Fangjiang then had rather some departures.
translations.idiomatic: The Fangjiang text, however, contained a number of departures from that norm.

sentences.zh: 至元始中，徵天下通小學者以百數，各令記字於庭中。

translations.literal: By Yuanshi period, over a hundred people proficient in elementary studies were summoned from across the realm, each ordered to write characters in the court.
translations.idiomatic: By the Yuanshi era, more than a hundred specialists in elementary philology were summoned from across the empire and ordered to write graphs in court examination.

sentences.zh: 揚雄取其有用者以作訓纂篇，順續蒼頡，又易蒼頡中重復之字，凡八十九章。

translations.literal: Yang Xiong selected what was useful and made Xunzuan pian, following Cangjie in sequence, and replaced repeated characters in Cangjie, in all 89 chapters.
translations.idiomatic: Yang Xiong selected useful material to compile Xunzuan, sequenced after Cangjie, and replaced repeated Cangjie graphs, producing 89 chapters.

sentences.zh: 臣復續揚雄作十二章，凡一百二章，無復字，六藝群書所載略備矣。

translations.literal: Your servant further continued Yang Xiong by making 12 chapters, in all 102 chapters, without repeated characters; what is carried in Six Arts and group books is roughly complete.
translations.idiomatic: I then extended Yang Xiong’s work by twelve further chapters, bringing the total to 102 with no repeated graphs, and covering nearly all characters found across the Six Arts corpus and related texts.

sentences.zh: 蒼頡多古字，俗師失其讀，宣帝時徵齊人能正讀者，張敞從受之，傳至外孫之子杜林，為作訓故，并列焉。

translations.literal: Cangjie has many archaic characters and vulgar teachers lost their readings. In Emperor Xuan’s time they summoned a Qi man able to correct readings; Zhang Chang learned from him, and transmission reached Du Lin, son of his daughter’s son, who made Xungu and listed it here.
translations.idiomatic: Because Cangjie contained many archaic forms, common instructors had lost proper readings. In Emperor Xuan’s reign, a specialist from Qi was summoned to restore pronunciations; Zhang Chang learned from him, and the line passed to his matrilineal grandson Du Lin, who produced a corrective exegesis included here.

sentences.zh: 凡六藝一百三家，三千一百二十三篇。

translations.literal: In total Six Arts: 103 families, 3,123 pian.
translations.idiomatic: Total Six Arts corpus: 103 lineages, 3,123 fascicles.

sentences.zh: 六藝之文：樂以和神，仁之表也；

translations.literal: As for Six Arts writings: music harmonizes spirits, manifestation of benevolence;
translations.idiomatic: Within the Six Arts, music harmonizes the numinous and manifests benevolence;

translations.literal: Odes rectify speech, function of righteousness;
translations.idiomatic: the Odes rectify language and enact righteousness;

sentences.zh: 禮以明體，明者著見，故無訓也；

translations.literal: Rites make clear embodied order; what is clear is evident, thus no gloss needed;
translations.idiomatic: Rites clarify social form and embodied order; what is evident requires little gloss;

translations.literal: Documents broaden hearing, art of knowledge;
translations.idiomatic: Documents broaden what rulers can hear, and so are a technique of knowledge;

translations.literal: Spring and Autumn judges affairs, token of trust.
translations.idiomatic: the Spring and Autumn judges events and serves as a token of moral credibility.

sentences.zh: 五者，蓋五常之道，相須而備，而易為之原。

translations.literal: These five are the way of the five constants, mutually requiring and completing one another, and the Changes is their source.
translations.idiomatic: These five constitute the Way of the five constants, each dependent on the others for completion, while the Changes stands as their root.

sentences.zh: 故曰「易不可見，則乾坤或幾乎息矣」，言與天地為終始也。

translations.literal: Therefore it says "If the Changes cannot be seen, then Qian and Kun perhaps almost cease," saying it forms beginning and end with Heaven and Earth.
translations.idiomatic: Hence the saying: "If the Changes were not visible, Qian and Kun would nearly cease" - meaning it shares origin and culmination with Heaven and Earth.

sentences.zh: 至於五學，世有變改，猶五行之更用事焉。

translations.literal: As for the five schools, generations have changes and revisions, like the five phases alternately taking charge.
translations.idiomatic: As for the five scholarly traditions, they shift across generations just as the Five Phases alternately take command.

sentences.zh: 古之學者耕且養，三年而通一藝，存其大體，玩經文而已，是故用日少而畜德多，三十而五經立也。

translations.literal: Ancient learners farmed while supporting themselves; in three years they mastered one art, preserving its large form and simply studying canonical text. Therefore they used fewer days and accumulated more virtue; by thirty the Five Classics were established.
translations.idiomatic: Ancient students farmed to sustain themselves and spent three years mastering a single art, focusing on fundamentals and close textual study. Thus they consumed less time, cultivated more virtue, and by thirty had firmly grounded the Five Classics.

sentences.zh: 後世經傳既已乖離，博學者又不思多聞闕疑之義，而務碎義逃難，便辭巧說，破壞形體；

translations.literal: In later ages, as classics and commentaries had diverged, broad learners no longer considered the principle of hearing much and suspending doubt; instead they pursued fragmented meanings and evasive difficulties, facile phrases and clever theories, destroying textual form.
translations.idiomatic: In later times, once classics and commentaries diverged, many "broad" scholars abandoned the principle of wide learning with suspended judgment, chasing instead atomized interpretations, evasive argument, and clever rhetoric that damaged textual integrity.

sentences.zh: 說五字之文，至於二三萬言。

translations.literal: To explain five characters of text, they reached twenty or thirty thousand words.
translations.idiomatic: They could spend twenty or thirty thousand words explaining a phrase of only five characters.

sentences.zh: 後進彌以馳逐，故幼童而守一藝，白首而後能言；

translations.literal: Later students increasingly raced after this, so as children they guarded one art and only in white-haired old age could then speak;
translations.idiomatic: Later students pursued this style ever more frantically: they specialized from youth and only spoke confidently when gray-haired.

sentences.zh: 安其所習，毀所不見，終以自蔽。

translations.literal: They settle in what they learned and slander what they have not seen, ending by self-obstruction.
translations.idiomatic: They cling to their own training, denounce what they have not studied, and end by blinding themselves.

translations.literal: This is the great affliction of scholars.
translations.idiomatic: This is the great disease of scholarship.

translations.literal: The sequence of Six Arts into nine kinds.
translations.idiomatic: This is the classification that sequences the Six Arts into nine categories.

sentences.zh: 右儒五十三家，八百三十六篇。

translations.literal: Right (Ru): 53 families, 836 pian.
translations.idiomatic: Confucian works total 53 lineages and 836 fascicles.

sentences.zh: 儒家者流，蓋出於司徒之官，助人君順陰陽明教化者也。

translations.literal: The Confucian stream likely comes from the office of Minister of Education, assisting rulers to accord with yin-yang and clarify transformation through teaching.
translations.idiomatic: The Confucian school likely arose from the old Minister of Education office. Its role is to help rulers align with cosmic order and make moral transformation clear through teaching.

sentences.zh: 游文於六經之中，留意於仁義之際，祖述堯舜，憲章文武，宗師仲尼，以重其言，於道最為高。

translations.literal: It roams in the Six Classics, focuses on the bounds of benevolence and righteousness, traces Yao and Shun, models Wen and Wu, and takes Confucius as ancestral master to give weight to its words; in the Way it is highest.
translations.idiomatic: It works within the Six Classics, centers on benevolence and righteousness, inherits Yao and Shun, models Kings Wen and Wu, and venerates Confucius as its master; in terms of doctrine, it stands at the highest level.

sentences.zh: 孔子曰：「如有所譽，其有所試。」

translations.literal: Confucius said: "If there is praise, there has been testing."
translations.idiomatic: Confucius said, "Where there is praise, there has first been testing."

sentences.zh: 唐虞之隆，殷周之盛，仲尼之業，已試之效者也。

translations.literal: The flourishing of Tang-Yu, the prosperity of Yin-Zhou, and the work of Confucius are effects already tested.
translations.idiomatic: The greatness of Tang-Yu, the prosperity of Yin and Zhou, and the achievement of Confucius are all results verified in practice.

sentences.zh: 然惑者既失精微，而辟者又隨時抑揚，違離道本，苟以譁眾取寵。

translations.literal: Yet the confused lost the subtle essence, and the heterodox further rose and fell with times, departing from the root of the Way, merely using clamor to win favor.
translations.idiomatic: Yet misguided interpreters lost the subtle core, and opportunists adjusted doctrines to fashion, drifting from the Way’s foundation and courting favor through noise.

sentences.zh: 後進循之，是以五經乖析，儒學浸衰，此辟儒之患。

translations.literal: Later learners followed this, therefore the Five Classics split apart and Confucian learning declined; this is the harm of deviant Confucians.
translations.idiomatic: Later scholars copied this habit. As a result, the Five Classics fractured and Confucian learning declined. This is the danger posed by deviant Confucianism.

translations.literal: 伊尹: 五十一 pian.
太公: 二百三十七 pian.

sentences.zh: 謀八十一篇，言七十一篇，兵八十五篇。

translations.idiomatic: Subdivisions: 81 fascicles of strategic plans, 71 of discursive sayings, and 85 of military material.

sentences.zh: 右道三十七家，九百九十三篇。

translations.literal: Right (Dao): 37 families, 993 pian.
translations.idiomatic: Daoist works total 37 lineages and 993 fascicles.

sentences.zh: 道家者流，蓋出於史官，歷記成敗存亡禍福古今之道，然後知秉要執本，清虛以自守，卑弱以自持，此君人南面之術也。

translations.literal: The Daoist stream likely comes from historians, recording paths of success-failure, survival-destruction, fortune-disaster, ancient and modern; then knowing to hold essentials and grasp roots, keep to purity and emptiness, and sustain oneself by humility and weakness - this is the art for rulers facing south.
translations.idiomatic: The Daoist current likely grew from historiographic practice, observing patterns of rise and fall, survival and ruin, fortune and calamity across ages. From this comes its method: hold fast to essentials, preserve emptiness and clarity, and sustain rule through humility and softness - an art suited to sovereigns.

sentences.zh: 合於堯之克攘，易之嗛嗛，一謙而四益，此其所長也。

translations.literal: It accords with Yao's ability to yield and with the Changes' modesty; one humility and four gains - this is its strength.
translations.idiomatic: Its strength lies in accord with Yao’s yielding spirit and the Changes’ ideal of modesty: one act of humility yielding fourfold benefit.

sentences.zh: 及放者為之，則欲絕去禮學，兼棄仁義，曰獨任清虛可以為治。

translations.literal: When unrestrained people practice it, they then wish to cut off rites and learning, and jointly discard benevolence and righteousness, saying solely relying on purity and emptiness can govern.
translations.idiomatic: But when extremists adopt it, they seek to abolish ritual and learning altogether, discard benevolence and righteousness, and claim that purity and emptiness alone can govern the state.

sentences.zh: 右陰陽二十一家，三百六十九篇。

translations.literal: Right (Yin-Yang): 21 families, 369 pian.
translations.idiomatic: Yin-Yang works total 21 lineages and 369 fascicles.

sentences.zh: 陰陽家者流，蓋出於羲和之官，敬順昊天，歷象日月星辰，敬授民時，此其所長也。

translations.literal: The Yin-Yang stream likely comes from the office of Xi and He, reverently following vast Heaven, calendrically imaging sun, moon, stars, and respectfully granting the seasons to the people - this is its strength.
translations.idiomatic: The Yin-Yang school likely derives from the ancient Xi-He calendrical office. Its strength is reverently aligning with Heaven by tracking the sun, moon, and stars and properly assigning seasonal timing to the people.

sentences.zh: 及拘者為之，則牽於禁忌，泥於小數，舍人事而任鬼神。

translations.literal: When constrained people practice it, they are then tied to taboos, stuck in petty calculations, abandoning human affairs and entrusting ghosts and spirits.
translations.idiomatic: In rigid hands, however, it degenerates into taboo-obsession and numerological pedantry, neglecting human governance in favor of spirits and omens.

sentences.zh: 右法十家，二百一十七篇。

translations.literal: Right (Legalists): 10 families, 217 pian.
translations.idiomatic: Legalist works total 10 lineages and 217 fascicles.

sentences.zh: 法家者流，蓋出於理官，信賞必罰，以輔禮制。

translations.literal: The Legalist stream likely comes from judicial officials, making rewards trustworthy and punishments certain to assist ritual institutions.
translations.idiomatic: The Legalist school likely arose from judicial administration, emphasizing reliable rewards and certain punishments to reinforce institutional order.

sentences.zh: 《易》曰「先王以明罰飭法」，此其所長也。

translations.literal: The Changes says, "Former kings used clear punishments to rectify laws"; this is its strength.
translations.idiomatic: As the Changes says, "Former kings clarified punishments to correct the law" - this is the school’s strength.

sentences.zh: 及刻者為之，則無教化，去仁愛，專任刑法而欲以致治，至於殘害至親，傷恩薄厚。

translations.literal: When harsh people practiced it, there was no teaching transformation; they cast off benevolence and love, exclusively used penal law and sought governance by it, leading to harming closest kin and thinning bonds of grace.
translations.idiomatic: But in harsh hands, it rejects moral education, abandons benevolence, and relies solely on punishment for rule - leading even to harm among close kin and the erosion of humane ties.

translations.literal: The School of Names likely comes from ritual officials.
translations.idiomatic: The School of Names likely derives from ritual administration.

sentences.zh: 古者名位不同，禮亦異數。

translations.literal: In antiquity, names and ranks differed, and rites likewise had different measures.
translations.idiomatic: In antiquity, titles and ranks were distinct, and ritual forms were correspondingly differentiated.

translations.literal: Confucius said: "It must be rectification of names!
translations.idiomatic: Confucius said, "What is essential is the rectification of names.

sentences.zh: 名不正則言不順，言不順則事不成。」

translations.literal: If names are not correct, speech is not smooth; if speech is not smooth, affairs are not accomplished."
translations.idiomatic: If names are not correct, language loses order; if language loses order, affairs cannot be completed."

translations.literal: This is its strength.

sentences.zh: 及譥者為之，則苟鉤鈲析亂而已。

translations.literal: When contentious sophists practiced it, they merely hooked, split, and confused distinctions.
translations.idiomatic: When contentious sophists practiced it, they reduced it to hair-splitting and conceptual confusion.

sentences.zh: 墨家者流，蓋出於清廟之守。

translations.literal: The Mohist stream likely comes from guardians of the ancestral temple.
translations.idiomatic: The Mohist school likely emerged from officials guarding the ancestral temple complex.

translations.literal: Thatch roofs and rough rafters, therefore they valued frugality;
translations.idiomatic: With thatched roofs and simple rafters, they prized frugality;

translations.literal: Nourishing Three Elders and Five Geng, therefore they practiced universal care;
translations.idiomatic: with institutions for honoring elders, they promoted impartial care;

translations.literal: Selecting men in great archery, therefore they elevated the worthy;
translations.idiomatic: with competitive selection rituals, they elevated worthiness;

translations.literal: In ancestral sacrifices, honoring strict fathers, therefore they honored spirits;
translations.idiomatic: with strict ancestral rites, they gave weight to spirits;

translations.literal: Following the four seasons in action, therefore they denied fixed fate;
translations.idiomatic: by acting in accord with seasons, they rejected fatalism;

sentences.zh: 以孝視天下，是以上同：此其所長也。

translations.literal: Viewing all-under-heaven through filiality, therefore they upheld alignment above: this is its strength.
translations.idiomatic: and by extending filial standards universally, they upheld hierarchical alignment. These are its strengths.

sentences.zh: 及蔽者為之，見儉之利，因以非禮，推兼愛之意，而不知別親疏。

translations.literal: When obstructed people practiced it, seeing frugality’s benefit they used it to reject rites, extending universal care’s meaning yet not knowing distinctions of closeness and distance.
translations.idiomatic: But in narrow interpretations, frugality was used to attack ritual itself, and universal care was pushed without regard to distinctions of intimacy and obligation.

translations.idiomatic: Diplomatic-persuasion works total 12 lineages and 107 fascicles.

sentences.zh: 從橫家者流，蓋出於行人之官。

translations.literal: The Vertical-Horizontal stream likely comes from the office of envoys.
translations.idiomatic: The Vertical-and-Horizontal school likely arose from the office of diplomatic envoys.

sentences.zh: 孔子曰：「誦詩三百，使於四方，不能專對，雖多亦奚以為？」

translations.literal: Confucius said: "Recite 300 Odes, yet when sent in all four directions cannot answer independently - though much, what use?"
translations.idiomatic: Confucius said, "One may recite all three hundred Odes, but if sent on mission cannot answer independently, what use is such learning?"

translations.literal: Again he said: "As envoy! As envoy!"
translations.idiomatic: He also said, "As envoy - one must truly be an envoy!"

sentences.zh: 言其當權事制宜，受命而不受辭，此其所長也。

translations.literal: This means they should weigh circumstances and adapt institutions, receiving mandate without receiving fixed wording; this is its strength.
translations.idiomatic: That means an envoy must judge circumstances and adapt policy, receiving a mission but not merely repeating scripted language. This is the school’s strength.

sentences.zh: 及邪人為之，則上詐諼而棄其信。

translations.literal: When crooked people practiced it, they then used deception above and abandoned trustworthiness.
translations.idiomatic: But in corrupt hands it became a craft of deception and abandonment of trust.

translations.idiomatic: The Miscellaneous school likely derives from policy-deliberation offices.

sentences.zh: 兼儒、墨，合名、法，知國體之有此，見王治之無不貫，此其所長也。

translations.literal: It combines Ru and Mo, unites Names and Law, knows state form has these, and sees kingly governance has nothing not interconnected; this is its strength.
translations.idiomatic: It integrates Confucian and Mohist ideas, combines the Schools of Names and Law, understands the structural plurality of the state, and views good kingship as integrative - this is its strength.

sentences.zh: 及盪者為之，則漫羨而無所歸心。

translations.literal: When unrestrained people practiced it, they then became diffuse and overflowing with no place for the heart to return.
translations.idiomatic: In unrestrained use, however, it turns diffuse and unfocused, leaving no stable center of commitment.

sentences.zh: 農家者流，蓋出於農稷之官。

translations.literal: The Agrarian stream likely comes from the offices of agriculture and millet.
translations.idiomatic: The Agrarian school likely arose from offices responsible for farming and grain production.

sentences.zh: 播百穀，勸耕桑，以足衣食，故八政一曰食，二曰貨。

translations.literal: Sowing hundred grains and encouraging plowing and mulberry to supply clothing and food; therefore among Eight Policies first is food, second is goods.
translations.idiomatic: Its doctrine is to sow the hundred grains and promote tillage and sericulture to secure food and clothing. Hence in the Eight Policies, food comes first and economic goods second.

sentences.zh: 孔子曰「所重民食」，此其所長也。

translations.literal: Confucius said, "What is valued is the people’s food"; this is its strength.
translations.idiomatic: Confucius said, "What matters most is the people’s food supply" - this is the school’s strength.

sentences.zh: 及鄙者為之，以為無所事聖王，欲使君臣並耕，誖上下之序。

translations.literal: When vulgar people practiced it, they thought there was no need to serve sage kings and wished ruler and ministers to farm together, violating the order of above and below.
translations.idiomatic: But in crude interpretations, it denied the role of sage rulership and demanded ruler and ministers plow alike, overturning hierarchical order.

sentences.zh: 右小說十五家，千三百八十篇。

translations.literal: Right (Minor narratives): 15 families, 1,380 pian.
translations.idiomatic: Minor-narrative works total 15 lineages and 1,380 fascicles.

sentences.zh: 小說家者流，蓋出於稗官。

translations.literal: The minor-narrative stream likely comes from petty officials.
translations.idiomatic: The minor-narrative school likely arose from petty reporting officials.

sentences.zh: 街談巷語，道聽塗說者之所造也。

translations.literal: Street talk and alley speech, rumors heard by roads and told by paths, are what they produced.
translations.idiomatic: Its materials are street gossip, alley talk, and hearsay picked up and retold on the roads.

sentences.zh: 孔子曰：「雖小道，必有可觀者焉，致遠恐泥，是以君子弗為也。」

translations.literal: Confucius said: "Though minor paths, there are surely things worth seeing there; pursuing far, I fear getting stuck in mud, thus the gentleman does not practice them."
translations.idiomatic: Confucius said, "Even minor paths have things worth observing, but to push them too far risks getting mired, so the gentleman does not make them his way."

translations.literal: Yet they are not entirely abolished.
translations.idiomatic: Yet they should not be wholly discarded.

sentences.zh: 閭里小知者之所及，亦使綴而不忘。

translations.literal: What small local knowers can reach should also be stitched and not forgotten.
translations.idiomatic: What local people of limited learning can preserve should still be gathered and not forgotten.

sentences.zh: 如或一言可采，此亦芻蕘狂夫之議也。

translations.literal: If perhaps one saying can be adopted, this too is the opinion of grass-cutters and rough men.
translations.idiomatic: If even one saying among them is useful, it is still worth preserving - like counsel from woodcutters and common men.

sentences.zh: 凡諸子百八十九家，四千三百二十四篇。

translations.literal: In total all masters: 189 families, 4,324 pian.
translations.idiomatic: Total for all masters’ literature: 189 lineages and 4,324 fascicles.

sentences.zh: 諸子十家，其可觀者九家而已。

translations.literal: Among the ten schools of masters, only nine are worth observing.
translations.idiomatic: Of the ten major philosophical schools, only nine are truly worth close study.

sentences.zh: 皆起於王道既微，諸侯力政，時君世主，好惡殊方，是以九家之術蠭出並作，各引一端，崇其所善，以此馳說，取合諸侯。

translations.literal: All arose when kingly way had weakened, feudal lords governed by force, and rulers in each age had differing likes and dislikes; therefore the arts of nine schools swarmed forth together, each drawing one end and honoring what it was good at, using this to race in persuasion and win acceptance by feudal lords.
translations.idiomatic: They all emerged after the kingly way had declined, when feudal lords ruled by force and each age’s rulers favored different things. Thus the nine schools surged forth, each advancing one aspect and exalting its own strength to persuade competing courts.

sentences.zh: 其言雖殊，辟猶水火，相滅亦相生也。

translations.literal: Though their words differ, they are like water and fire: mutually overcoming yet also mutually generating.
translations.idiomatic: Though their doctrines differ, they are like water and fire - they oppose one another, yet also generate one another.

sentences.zh: 仁之與義，敬之與和，相反而皆相成也。

translations.literal: Benevolence and righteousness, reverence and harmony, are opposite yet all mutually complete.
translations.idiomatic: Benevolence and righteousness, reverence and harmony: opposites in form, yet mutually completing in function.

sentences.zh: 《易》曰：「天下同歸而殊塗，一致而百慮。」

translations.literal: The Changes says: "All under heaven return to one though by different roads, arrive at one though with a hundred concerns."
translations.idiomatic: As the Changes says: "All under heaven returns to one destination by different roads, and reaches one end through a hundred reflections."

sentences.zh: 今異家者各推所長，窮知究慮，以明其指，雖有蔽短，合其要歸，亦六經之支與流裔。

translations.literal: Now each differing school pushes its strengths, exhausting knowledge and thought to clarify its aim. Though it has obstructions and shortcomings, combining their essential return, they are also branches and downstream descendants of the Six Classics.
translations.idiomatic: Each school presses its own strengths and drives inquiry to its limits to clarify its intent. Though each has blind spots, their shared essentials still form branches and offshoots of the Six Classics.

sentences.zh: 使其人遭明王聖主，得其所折中，皆股肱之材已。

translations.literal: If their people encountered enlightened kings and sage rulers and obtained what was to be balanced, all would be talent of arms and legs.
translations.idiomatic: If thinkers of these schools met an enlightened ruler and were properly balanced and employed, all could become indispensable state talent.

sentences.zh: 仲尼有言：「禮失而求諸野。」

translations.literal: Confucius said: "When rites are lost, seek them in the wilds."
translations.idiomatic: Confucius said, "When ritual is lost, seek it in the outlying places."

sentences.zh: 方今去聖久遠，道術缺廢，無所更索，彼九家者，不猶瘉於野乎？

translations.literal: Now far removed long from the sages, arts of the Way are lacking and abandoned, with nowhere else to seek; are those nine schools not still better than the wilds?
translations.idiomatic: Now that we are long removed from the sages and many arts of the Way lie broken, where else can we search? Are these nine schools not still better than utter ignorance?

sentences.zh: 若能修六藝之術，而觀此九家之言，舍短取長，則可以通萬方之略矣。

translations.literal: If one can cultivate the arts of the Six Classics and observe the words of these nine schools, discarding short and taking long, then one can understand strategies of ten-thousand directions.
translations.idiomatic: If one masters the Six Classics while studying these nine schools - rejecting what is weak and taking what is strong - one can grasp strategy across the whole world.
